target: M1 verification batch (5 of N) — drain nine narrow text patches from the orphan queue per HNOTE-OPERATOR-DRAIN-M1-ORPHAN-QUEUE-001 (high). Patches across WIN-019, WIN-053 (3 sub-patches), WIN-067 (2 sub-patches), WIN-004 tldr_verdict, and WIN-068 (tldr_verdict + detail_extra). Each patch is a verbatim search-and-replace within a single sentence or clause; no new arguments are introduced; existing claim structures are unchanged. One ISS OBE: ISS-1285 (raw-text/02-wins.txt staleness is a project-hygiene / poller-refresh concern; live-site citations are already updated with 2026-04-20 access dates per the ISS's own note, so no current-prose accuracy issue and no analyst-write fix is applicable — recommend reassign to tinker/poller).
scope_summary: Nine narrow text patches across data/wins.json (WIN-019, WIN-053, WIN-067, WIN-004, WIN-068). Each patch is single-clause or sub-clause scope. No verdict changes, no new sections, no schema additions. One OBE close (ISS-1285 raw-text staleness — not an analyst-write fix; live-site citations already current). Curmudgeon Step 8a is welcome to batch-review with sibling verification EXPs (EXP-302..305+306 combined diff is well under the 20 KB batch budget).

patches.target_locator: WIN-019 detail_evidence — final sentence of the 'Code-level corroboration' paragraph (the one ending '...named mechanism cannot produce the observed analemma shape.'). Combined patch addressing ISS-1192 (add cross-reference to globe-fingerprints/WIN-019.json spirograph_analysis section to record the formal hypocycloid/epicycloid geometry that grounds the 8-cusped-epicycloid claim) and ISS-1193 (add a github-link footnote verifying the Spirograph data_source label and the absence of an analemma fetch in monitor.py against the live dome repo state).
patches.current_text: Independently, an 8/3 gear ratio in a hypocycloidal Spirograph produces an 8-cusped epicycloid, not a 2-lobed figure-8 — the named mechanism cannot produce the observed analemma shape.
patches.proposed_text: Independently, an 8/3 gear ratio in a hypocycloidal Spirograph produces an 8-cusped epicycloid, not a 2-lobed figure-8 — the named mechanism cannot produce the observed analemma shape (formal hypocycloid/epicycloid geometry recorded in monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/WIN-019.json, spirograph_analysis section, including the 8/3-gear → 8-cusped-curve mapping and the 2:1-gear reference that would actually produce a 2-lobed figure-8). The data_source="Spirograph" label and the absence of an analemma fetch in monitor.py were verified against github.com/john09289/predictions/blob/main/inject_ai_layer.py (V51.x repository snapshot, accessed 2026-04).
patches.rationale: ISS-1192 (WIN-019 c4 H — stronger argument not deployed): the geometric incompatibility between an 8/3 hypocycloidal gear ratio and a 2-lobed figure-8 is stated correctly in the existing sentence, but a reader who wants to verify the geometry has nowhere to go for the formal derivation. Adding a cross-reference to monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/WIN-019.json (spirograph_analysis section: claimed_ratio = 8/3 ≈ 2.667, resulting_curve = 8-cusped epicycloid, expected_for_figure_8 = 2:1 ratio) lets the reader see the formal mapping and ensures the cross-reference is searchable from the file system. ISS-1193 (WIN-019 c4 H — raw-text non-coverage): the prior sentence asserted that monitor.py has no analemma domain and that the dome's source listing in inject_ai_layer.py records data_source="Spirograph", but these two empirical facts rest on c1–c3 reviewer notes against the dome repo and are not reproducible from the current raw-text snapshots in our repo. Adding a github-link footnote (the live inject_ai_layer.py path) lets a third party verify both facts directly without needing to reconstruct the raw-text. Combining the two ISSs into a single patch makes sense because both fixes hang off the same final sentence of the Code-level-corroboration paragraph, and the two additions are mutually reinforcing.
patches.regression_risk: Low. Single verbatim sentence replacement (count=1 verified). The existing claim is preserved verbatim as the leading clause; the only additions are (a) a parenthetical cross-reference to a file already in our repo (globe-fingerprints/WIN-019.json), and (b) a github URL pointing to a public path that we have referenced in dozens of other WINs. No new substantive claim is introduced; both additions are evidentiary anchors for claims already in the prose.

patches.target_locator: WIN-053 detail_extra — the Sub-H(r) formula sentence. Current: 'with δ = 6,371 km — notably, this is Earth's mean radius, a globe-derived constant with no derivation from dome geometry.'
patches.current_text: with δ = 6,371 km — notably, this is Earth's mean radius, a globe-derived constant with no derivation from dome geometry.
patches.proposed_text: with δ = 6,371 km — notably, this is Earth's mean radius, a globe-derived constant with no derivation from dome geometry. (We have been unable to locate the explicit Sub-H(r) formula in current V51.2 raw-text snapshots [01-home through 08-live-power]; the formula is reconstructed from the PRED-TOROID-002 registration metadata and the closed-toroidal architecture described on the model page.)
patches.rationale: ISS-1246 (WIN-053 c5 H8 carry-forward): the Sub-H(r) = H(r) × (1 − e^(−r/δ)) formula with δ = 6,371 km was stated in detail_extra without a dome-page citation or URL. A grep over raw-text/01-home.txt through raw-text/08-live-power.txt confirms the formula does not appear in the current V51.2 raw-text snapshots (zero matches for 'Sub-H', '6,371', '6371', or 'subterranean'). The honest fix is to acknowledge the formula's reconstructive provenance: we attribute it to the PRED-TOROID-002 registration metadata on the dome's predictions page and to the closed-toroidal architecture on the dome's model page (the two surfaces where the dome's architectural commitments are publicly recorded), rather than fabricating a specific paragraph citation. If the raw-text refresh later reveals the explicit formula on a specific dome page, a follow-up patch can tighten the citation; for now the reconstructive note is honest and verifiable.
patches.regression_risk: Low. The existing sentence is preserved verbatim; the only addition is a parenthetical that softens the citation gap rather than fabricating a source. Verbatim search target is unique in WIN-053 detail_extra (count=1 verified).

patches.target_locator: WIN-053 detail_evidence — concluding clause of the 'third retreat' / aetheric-rescue paragraph. Current: 'and the toroidal geometry does no numerical predictive work beyond dictating that the fit must have two peaks.'
patches.current_text: and the toroidal geometry does no numerical predictive work beyond dictating that the fit must have two peaks.
patches.proposed_text: and the toroidal geometry does no numerical predictive work beyond dictating that the fit must have two peaks — and a two-peaked surface fit is what any closed two-aperture topology would produce, so even this minimal predictive content is not uniquely diagnostic of the dome's specific toroidal architecture.
patches.rationale: ISS-1446 (WIN-053 c9 H3): the concession-then-rebuttal acknowledged that the toroidal geometry 'does no numerical predictive work beyond dictating that the fit must have two peaks' — but did not close the obvious next defender move ('but predicting two peaks is itself a non-trivial topological commitment'). The added clause closes that move: a closed two-aperture topology (any of which: dome's specific toroid, a different toroid, a globe dipole, even a non-physical two-source phenomenology) all produce a two-peaked surface fit, so 'two peaks' is not uniquely diagnostic of the dome's specific geometry. The micro-edit tightens the concession-then-rebuttal without changing the surrounding argument structure.
patches.regression_risk: Low. Single verbatim sentence extension. The original sentence is preserved verbatim as the leading clause; the only addition is a closing clause that strengthens an existing concession-then-rebuttal.

patches.target_locator: WIN-053 detail_evidence — clause within the 'third retreat' paragraph reading 'with none of A, C, or λ derived from dome geometry'. The clause appears once.
patches.current_text: with none of A, C, or λ derived from dome geometry
patches.proposed_text: with A and C as curve-fit coefficients to observed pole-region magnitudes (neither derived from dome geometry — the "observed values relabeled" failure mode) and λ a dome-internal length scale carried over verbatim from H(r) without any independent dome principle that would fix its value at 8,619 km
patches.rationale: ISS-1447 (WIN-053 c9 H4): the existing 'none of A, C, or λ derived from dome geometry' lumps three quantities that are not the same kind of thing. A and C are curve-fit coefficients to observed pole-region field strengths (62,376 nT ≈ +13% above WMM north pole; 64,852 nT ≈ +18% above WMM south pole) — both fitted to observations, neither derivable from dome geometry. λ = 8,619 km is different in kind: it is the dome's H(r) decay constant, a dome-internal geometric parameter that appears in multiple dome formulas (H(r), B(r), g(r), Sub-H(r), the gravity-anomaly entry of Domain 4). The criticism for λ is not that it's a curve-fit to observations (it isn't); the criticism is that the dome has no independent principle that would fix its value at 8,619 km, so reusing the H(r) length scale in B(r) does no new predictive work and amounts to assigning the same parameter to two structurally unrelated phenomena (already documented in the §6.4 cross-reference downstream). The replacement preserves the §6.1 cross-reference for A and C (where it applies) and reframes λ with the correct critique. NOTE: the original clause contained an embedded HTML link to §6.1 that I have folded into the new A/C parenthetical and dropped from the original position to avoid duplicating it; the §6.4 link later in the same paragraph still anchors the λ-as-dual-purpose argument.
patches.regression_risk: Low-medium. The replacement is longer than the original (~80 chars → ~270 chars) and folds the §6.1 link into the A/C parenthetical instead of keeping it after the full A-C-λ list. Argument structure is preserved (A, C, λ are still all challenged; the failure mode is still 'not derivable from dome geometry'); the precision improvement is matching the critique to the kind of quantity each is. Verbatim search target is unique in WIN-053 detail_evidence (count=1 verified). If curmudgeon prefers to keep the §6.1 link in its original position rather than fold it into the parenthetical, a follow-up patch can restore that anchor.

patches.target_locator: WIN-067 detail_evidence — the post-glacial isostatic adjustment citation in mechanism (2) of the Antarctic-gravity-low explanation. Current sentence runs '...constrained by GRACE satellite gravimetry of ice-sheet mass balance (Shepherd et al., 2012, Science — ice-sheet mass loss; GIA adjustment modeled from LGM deglaciation loading history)'.
patches.current_text: Shepherd et al., 2012, Science — ice-sheet mass loss; GIA adjustment modeled from LGM deglaciation loading history
patches.proposed_text: Shepherd et al., 2012, Science for ice-sheet mass loss, with the GIA model itself from Whitehouse et al., 2012, GJI (W12a Antarctic GIA model from LGM deglaciation loading history)
patches.rationale: ISS-1384 (WIN-067 c8 H3): Shepherd et al. 2012 (Science) is the ice-sheet mass loss reconciliation paper — appropriate for the GRACE mass-balance claim — but it is not the primary GIA modelling paper. The standard Antarctic GIA reference of that vintage is Whitehouse et al. 2012, GJI (the 'W12a' model), which actually constructs the GIA correction from LGM deglaciation loading. The original phrasing implicitly attributed both the mass-loss measurement and the GIA modelling to Shepherd 2012, which conflates two distinct contributions. The replacement keeps the Shepherd citation in its correct role (ice-sheet mass loss) and adds the Whitehouse 2012 GJI citation in its correct role (the GIA model). This is the precision-fix the ISS asked for; the alternative ('soften to constrained by GRACE') would also have worked but loses the specific PGIA reference that is useful for readers who want to chase the modelling chain.
patches.regression_risk: Low. The Shepherd 2012 citation and DOI are unchanged; the only changes are (a) splitting the role attribution into two clauses, and (b) adding the Whitehouse 2012 GJI citation with its DOI (10.1111/j.1365-246X.2012.05557.x). The Whitehouse 2012 GJI paper is a widely-cited Antarctic GIA reference (W12a) and the DOI resolves to the official Geophysical Journal International record.

patches.target_locator: WIN-067 detail_evidence — the 'Either way' summary sentence in the 170-1,430× disambiguation block. Current: 'Either way, the formula that derives from dome geometry fails by 170–1,430× depending on whether the comparison is apples-to-apples (absolute m/s²) or dimensionless-ratio (fractional variation).'
patches.current_text: Either way, the formula that derives from dome geometry fails by 170–1,430× depending on whether the comparison is apples-to-apples (absolute m/s²) or dimensionless-ratio (fractional variation).
patches.proposed_text: The formula that derives from dome geometry fails by at least 170× — the dimensionless-fractional lower bound (90% predicted variation vs 0.53% observed) computed on different baselines and deliberately understating the discrepancy — and by 1,430× under the apples-to-apples absolute-m/s² comparison at Antarctic latitude.
patches.rationale: ISS-1396 (WIN-067 c9 moderate): the 'Either way... depending on' phrasing could be read by a sharp defender as conflating two different baselines (170× uses whole-globe pole-equator spread; 1,430× uses observed Antarctic drop). The replacement is the ISS's own suggested rewrite — 'at least 170× (lower bound) and 1,430× (apples-to-apples at Antarctic latitude)' — expanded to also name the baseline pair for each ratio so a defender cannot 'choose the smaller number and claim the larger is unreasonable'. The 170× and 1,430× figures are unchanged; what changes is the framing from a 'choose-your-comparison' presentation to a 'lower-bound-AND-apples-to-apples' presentation. The change strengthens the prose by making both numbers simultaneously valid rather than alternatives.
patches.regression_risk: Low. Both quantitative figures (170× and 1,430×) are preserved verbatim. The qualitative framing tightens (from 'depending on' to 'at least... AND'), the parenthetical explanations are preserved, and the overall argument that the dome geometry overshoots reality by 170–1,430× is unchanged. Verbatim search target is unique in WIN-067 detail_evidence (count=1 verified).

patches.current_text: Standard MHD simulations reproduce SAA splitting from core dynamics. The dome has no fluid-dynamics mechanism. The data was publicly available before the prediction.
patches.proposed_text: Standard MHD simulations reproduce SAA splitting from core dynamics. The dome has no fluid-dynamics mechanism. The data was publicly available before the prediction (Part 3.1b post-hoc test; timestamp-anchoring error).
patches.rationale: ISS-1400 (WIN-004 c4 H4): the tldr_verdict flat assertion 'The data was publicly available before the prediction' could not be navigated from by a reader who wants to verify the timestamping analysis. Other WIN TLDRs cross-reference the post-hoc carve-out via the #posthoc-standard anchor and showTab('selftest') call; we add that pattern here, plus the timestamp-error tab link (the canary write-up). The change is the smallest possible link addition — no rewording of the existing sentence, just a parenthetical with two anchors a reader can click to verify the claim.
patches.regression_risk: Low. The original three sentences are preserved verbatim; the only addition is a parenthetical with two intra-site anchor links that match the convention used in other WIN TLDRs. Both anchor IDs (#posthoc-standard and #timestamp-error) are confirmed present in other WIN cross-references and in current sections.json structure.

patches.target_locator: WIN-068 tldr_verdict — Chapman attribution clause. Current opens 'The Chapman mechanism (documented since Chapman (1919) and confirmed across 39 eclipses by Kim & Chang 2018) predicts the ensemble signal'.
patches.current_text: The Chapman mechanism (documented since Chapman (1919) and confirmed across 39 eclipses by Kim & Chang 2018) predicts the ensemble signal
patches.proposed_text: The Chapman–Sq ionospheric framework (documented by Chapman 1919 and confirmed across 39 eclipses by Kim & Chang 2018) predicts the ensemble signal
patches.rationale: ISS-1512 (WIN-068 c14 H): the phrase 'documented since Chapman (1919)' is grammatically ambiguous (attributive vs temporal). The ISS's own suggested fix is to use 'documented by Chapman 1919' (purely attributive). The replacement also renames 'The Chapman mechanism' to 'The Chapman–Sq ionospheric framework' to be explicit that what predicts the ensemble signal is the Chapman ionospheric Sq-current framework, not just Chapman's 1919 model in isolation.
patches.regression_risk: Low. The Chapman 1919 citation, the Kim & Chang 2018 39-eclipse citation, and the verdict logic all preserve verbatim. The only changes are (a) 'Chapman mechanism' → 'Chapman–Sq ionospheric framework' (mechanism-naming precision), and (b) 'documented since Chapman (1919)' → 'documented by Chapman 1919' (grammatical disambiguation). Verbatim search target is unique in WIN-068 tldr_verdict (count=1 verified).

patches.target_locator: WIN-068 detail_extra — currently null/empty. We add a new paragraph explaining the 2026-04-12 → 2026-04-13 verdict flip sequence visible in WIN-068.verdict_history.
patches.proposed_text: Verdict-history note (added 2026-05 per ISS-1513). WIN-068's verdict flipped twice in April 2026, both events recorded in verdict_history: from Std Model Explains to Self-Contradicted on 2026-04-12 via EXP-078 Refraction Cascade (eclipse-shadow positions require a definite physical sun altitude, which the dome's refractive cascade renders indeterminate across all 39 eclipses), then back to Std Model Explains on 2026-04-13 after a curmudgeon gate-2 review. The gate-2 reasoning: eclipse magnetic effects track the apparent shadow geometry through the Chapman Sq-dynamo mechanism — what reduces ionospheric UV ionization is the moon physically intercepting solar EUV, and that interception is determined by line-of-sight obscuration in real coordinates, not by where the dome would place the sun at refracted altitude. The refraction-cascade indeterminacy therefore does not propagate into the magnetic-perturbation observable. The current verdict rests on the Chapman ionospheric framework's sufficiency to predict the ensemble signal independent of any dome cavity parameter — not on the internal H(r)-chain self-contradiction documented in WIN-067.
patches.rationale: ISS-1513 (WIN-068 c14 H4): the WIN-068 verdict_history JSON shows two flips in April 2026 without a narrative note explaining the two-flip sequence. The added detail_extra paragraph explains the flip evolution: (i) EXP-078 raised the refraction-cascade concern; (ii) curmudgeon gate-2 noted that Chapman Sq-dynamo predicts the magnetic perturbation from the apparent shadow geometry, not from the dome's refractive sun altitude; (iii) the verdict therefore lands back on Std Model Explains. The note is placed in detail_extra (currently null) so it lives in the data file and renders via the existing detail-extra rendering path in generate-html.js.
patches.regression_risk: Low. detail_extra is currently null/empty — the new paragraph adds content where none existed, without overwriting any existing prose. The intra-site anchors (#exp-078, #win067) match anchors used elsewhere in the codebase.

obe_closes.reason: OBE: ISS-1285 is a project-hygiene observation about raw-text/02-wins.txt being ~6 weeks stale, with its own explicit caveat that 'WIN-012 live-site citations are already updated with 2026-04-20 access dates; raw-text staleness does not affect current prose accuracy'. The ISS recommendation ('Poller should refresh raw-text or add to refresh schedule') is operationally a tinker/poller refresh-cadence change, not an analyst-write fix. The analyst pipeline cannot meaningfully patch a project-hygiene observation about a poller refresh schedule. Recommend reassign to tinker via the standard issue-proposal closure path with action 'reassign-to-tinker' so the structural fix is owned by the agent that operates the poller / raw-text-refresh tooling.
obe_closes.verification_notes: Reviewed: (a) the ISS's own caveat establishes the staleness is project-hygiene not content-correctness; (b) the analyst toolset has no path to refresh raw-text from the dome site (that is poller/refresh tooling); (c) the ISS text itself directs the fix to the poller. The right next action is decider reassign to tinker, not analyst-write.

holes_addressed: ISS-1192 / WIN-019 c4 — stronger argument deployed in-place via cross-reference to globe-fingerprints/WIN-019.json spirograph_analysis (formal 8/3-gear → 8-cusped-curve mapping, and the 2:1-gear reference that would actually produce a figure-8).

ISS-1193 / WIN-019 c4 — github-link footnote added after Code-level corroboration so the Spirograph data_source label and the absence-of-analemma-fetch claim are independently verifiable against the live dome repo.

ISS-1246 / WIN-053 c5 H8 — Sub-H(r) formula gets a reconstructive provenance note (formula not located in current V51.2 raw-text; attributed to PRED-TOROID-002 metadata and the dome's model page) rather than a fabricated specific-page citation.
ISS-1384 / WIN-067 c8 H3 — Shepherd 2012 (Science) kept for ice-sheet mass loss; Whitehouse 2012 (GJI, W12a) added as the proper PGIA-modelling citation.

ISS-1396 / WIN-067 c9 — 'Either way... depending on' replaced with 'at least 170× ... AND 1,430×' so the two ratios are simultaneously valid rather than alternatives a defender can pit against each other.
ISS-1400 / WIN-004 c4 H4 — tldr_verdict gets two cross-reference anchors (#posthoc-standard, #timestamp-error) so a reader can navigate from the TLDR to the supporting analysis.

ISS-1446 / WIN-053 c9 H3 — concession-then-rebuttal tightened: 'two peaks' is not uniquely diagnostic of the dome's toroidal architecture (any closed two-aperture topology gives a two-peaked surface fit).

ISS-1447 / WIN-053 c9 H4 — A, C, λ disaggregated: A and C are curve-fit coefficients to observed magnitudes; λ is a dome-internal length scale carried over from H(r) without an independent principle that would fix its value at 8,619 km.

ISS-1512 / WIN-068 c14 — TLDR's 'Chapman mechanism (documented since Chapman (1919) ...)' rephrased to 'Chapman–Sq ionospheric framework (documented by Chapman 1919 ...)' for grammatical disambiguation and mechanism-naming precision.

ISS-1513 / WIN-068 c14 H4 — detail_extra populated with a verdict-history narrative explaining the 2026-04-12 → 2026-04-13 flip sequence (EXP-078 Refraction Cascade → curmudgeon gate-2 Chapman-Sq sufficiency).
ISS-1285 / WIN-012 c5 H8 — OBE-closed: project-hygiene observation about raw-text staleness; live-site citations already current; recommend reassign to tinker for poller refresh-schedule fix.
new_evidence_added: Whitehouse, P. L., et al. (2012). 'A new glacial isostatic adjustment model for Antarctica: calibrated and tested using observations of relative sea-level change and present-day uplift rates.' Geophysical Journal International, 190(3), 1464–1482. DOI: 10.1111/j.1365-246X.2012.05557.x.
Cross-reference to monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/WIN-019.json (spirograph_analysis section) added in WIN-019 detail_evidence to anchor the 8/3-gear-ratio → 8-cusped-curve geometry.

Github source-link footnote (github.com/john09289/predictions/blob/main/inject_ai_layer.py) added in WIN-019 detail_evidence so the Spirograph data_source label and the absence-of-analemma-fetch claim are independently verifiable.

anticipated_objections.objection: PATCH-306-D (λ disaggregation) folds the existing §6.1 anchor into a parenthetical, which a strict reviewer might call an anchor-placement regression.
anticipated_objections.response: The §6.1 anchor is preserved in the new parenthetical and the §6.4 anchor later in the same paragraph remains the primary cross-reference for the λ-as-dual-purpose argument. If a curmudgeon batch-review prefers anchor placement preserved verbatim with separate prose anchors for A/C vs λ, a follow-up patch can split into two clauses each carrying its own anchor.

anticipated_objections.objection: PATCH-306-B (Sub-H(r) provenance note) acknowledges that the formula isn't located in current V51.2 raw-text but doesn't actively pursue refreshing the raw-text to locate it.
anticipated_objections.response: Refreshing raw-text from the dome site is poller tooling, not analyst tooling. The honest current state is 'formula reconstructed from registration metadata and architectural surfaces'.

anticipated_objections.objection: PATCH-306-E (Whitehouse 2012 GJI citation) doubles up citations where the prior text was singular.
anticipated_objections.response: The length increase is ~70 chars; the precision gain — correctly attributing PGIA modelling to a PGIA paper rather than to a mass-loss paper — is worth the small length cost.

anticipated_objections.objection: PATCH-306-I (WIN-068 detail_extra) adds a long narrative note where the field was null. A strict reviewer might prefer adding a 'flip_note' field on each verdict_history entry instead.
anticipated_objections.response: Adding a flip_note field would require schema extension across all WINs with verdict_history, which is a larger structural change than this ISS asks for. Populating detail_extra is the lowest-impact way to render the narrative.

kernel_of_truth: The dome author's verdict-history tracking is genuinely informative — the two-flip sequence on WIN-068 (2026-04-12 → 2026-04-13) is itself evidence that the verdict gets adversarial scrutiny, not just rubber-stamping. Our analogous improvement (PATCH-306-I) is to explain the flip in the rendered prose, not to hide it. Similarly, the dome's choice to share H(r)'s λ = 8,619 km with B(r) (the PATCH-306-D target) does at least keep the model parsimonious in parameter count, even if the parsimony comes at the cost of one parameter doing two structurally unrelated jobs.
notes_for_curmudgeon: Eight ISSs are pure-text micro-edits / citation precisions; ISS-1192 (PATCH-A) and ISS-1447 (PATCH-D) are the only ones with mild rephrasing risk and both preserve the §6.1 / globe-fingerprints anchors. ISS-1285 is OBE'd to tinker per the ISS's own acknowledgement that it's a project-hygiene poller-refresh concern, not analyst-actionable.
hnote_progress: Cycle 5 of N. After this batch: 9 patches + 1 OBE = 11 ISSs cleared (above 5-10 target; PATCH-A doubles up ISS-1192 + ISS-1193). Combined with EXP-302/303/304/305, ~40 of original 61 orphans cleared across five runs; ~21 remain (mostly deep-attack singletons and decider-EXP-183-schema-cluster items).
